message:
feat(heredoc): add tracing instrumentation for debugging (git_safety_guard-tlog)

Add the `tracing` crate and comprehensive instrumentation to the heredoc
detection pipeline for debugging and monitoring.

## Dependencies Added

- `tracing = "0.1"` - Core tracing API
- `tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }` - Log
  subscriber with env-filter support for RUST_LOG

## Instrumented Functions

### Tier 1: check_triggers()
- `#[instrument]` with command length field
- DEBUG: logs when heredoc/inline script indicator detected
- TRACE: logs fast-path allow when no trigger found

### Tier 2: extract_content()
- `#[instrument]` with command length and timeout_ms fields
- WARN: logs when input exceeds size limit (with actual/limit values)
- WARN: logs when binary content detected (with reason)
- TRACE: logs "no content found" path
- WARN: logs "skipped" path with skip count
- DEBUG: logs "content extracted" with count and elapsed_us
- DEBUG: logs "partial extraction with skips" with both counts

### Shell Command Extraction: extract_shell_commands()
- `#[instrument]` with content length field
- TRACE: logs when empty content provided
- DEBUG: logs AST analysis completion with elapsed_us and command count

## Usage

Enable tracing via RUST_LOG environment variable:

```bash
# Enable heredoc tracing at debug level
RUST_LOG=destructive_command_guard::heredoc=debug dcg check

# Enable all dcg tracing at info level
RUST_LOG=destructive_command_guard=info dcg check

# Full trace (very verbose)
RUST_LOG=destructive_command_guard=trace dcg check
```

## Log Levels per Spec

- ERROR: Reserved for parsing failures, timeout exceeded (to be added)
- WARN: Skip reasons (size limit, binary content, extraction failures)
- INFO: Reserved for heredoc detected, command blocked/allowed
- DEBUG: Pattern matches, timing details, extraction counts
- TRACE: Fast-path allows, empty content, detailed flow

Implements git_safety_guard-tlog.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
